message:
refactor(secrets): deep cx threading through scan_storage_*_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Twelfth caller-migration tick in the strangler-fig phase.

Two prerequisite storage cx-first siblings added first:
- `record_secret_scan_report_with_cx(cx, record)`
- `latest_secret_scan_report_with_cx(cx, scope_hash)`

Storage cx-first coverage now at 63/140 methods.

Secrets module migrated:

1. `scan_storage_incremental_with_cx`:
   - storage.latest_secret_scan_report → latest_secret_scan_report_with_cx
   - storage.record_secret_scan_report → record_secret_scan_report_with_cx

2. `scan_storage_from_with_cx` (private helper shared with
   `scan_storage_with_cx`):
   - storage.scan_segments → scan_segments_with_cx (inside the
     batch loop, already gated by a per-iteration
     `cx.checkpoint()`)

The doc comment on `scan_storage_with_cx` which previously noted
"storage._with_cx which does not yet exist (147 storage async fns
without cx)" is stale now that storage is at 63/140. The
per-iteration seam + cx-first `scan_segments` now combine so
caller cancellation propagates all the way into storage's
pre-flight checkpoint on every batch.

Legacy `scan_storage`, `scan_storage_incremental`, and private
`scan_storage_from` preserved unchanged.

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features asupersync-runtime): 72/72
secrets tests pass, including
`scan_storage_with_cx_matches_legacy_with_matches`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
